summary.intent: Fix all review comments on PR #476 in the moltis repo, including a P1 bug where the datetime system message was not re-injected into the context when a ContextWindowExceeded error triggered inline compaction and retry.
summary.outcome: The datetime injection was added to the retry path at line 6618 in crates/chat/src/lib.rs, compiled successfully, tests passed, and the fix was committed and pushed.

repo: Anthropic requires alternating user/assistant messages, so the merge-all-system-messages behavior must be preserved for that provider.
Prompt-cache stability for Anthropic via cache_control breakpoints is not yet implemented.
For local LLMs and OpenAI-compatible providers, trailing system messages can remain in-place in the message list to keep the system prompt prefix stable.

The ContextWindowExceeded retry path in crates/chat/src/lib.rs rebuilds chat history via values_to_chat_messages but historically skipped datetime injection, diverging from the initial chat_history path.

code.finding: Initial chat_history path correctly appends the datetime system message.
Retry path after ContextWindowExceeded only called values_to_chat_messages and skipped datetime injection — fixed in this session.

workflow: Review comments were replied to and resolved via CLI (gh) after pushing the fix.
Compilation and test checks were run before committing the fix.

friction: The datetime injection bug in the retry path was a non-obvious gap — the two code paths (initial and retry) diverged silently with no compile-time or test enforcement.
open_items: Prompt-cache stability for Anthropic via cache_control breakpoints is noted as future work.
